steps.title: Spot the replication pattern
steps.content: The passage describes how the geographic language distribution from China—Mandarin in the north, Cantonese/Fuzhounese in the south—replicates in New York City when those same regional groups immigrate: northerners settle in Queens, southerners in Manhattan.

steps.title: Match the inference
steps.content:
Choice B directly expresses this: regional language patterns from an origin country can replicate in a destination country when immigrants settle.

A and C introduce topics never discussed (language change rates, linguistic variation), while D's correlation between country size and language count is unrelated to the pattern shown.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
